header: BLAKE3 per the spec, with bug-catching cases targeting chunk boundaries, tree structure (balanced and unbalanced), chunk counter values, output-stream extension, and the keyed-hash mode.
Each non-KAT test's targets[] lists the implementation bugs it is designed to catch.

Ktv.description: Known-answer test from the BLAKE3 spec or cross-checked against @noble/hashes.

BlockBoundary.description: Input length is a multiple of the 64-byte block size, within a single chunk.

ChunkBoundary.description: Input length is at or near a 1024-byte chunk boundary.

ChunkBlockFlags.description: Each chunk-internal block has specific CHUNK_START / CHUNK_END flag combinations on its compression.

RootFlag.description: The ROOT flag is set on (and only on) the final output-producing compression.

TreeStart.description: Input length first exceeds chunk_size; triggers the binary-tree path.

TreeBalanced.description: Input has 2^N chunks; the tree is perfectly balanced.

TreeUnbalanced.description: Input has a non-power-of-2 number of chunks; tree is unbalanced and requires specific tree-construction rules.

ChunkCounter.description: Each chunk gets a cumulative 64-bit counter starting at 0; counter is mixed into the compression.

ExtendedOutput.description: Output longer than 32 bytes uses the XOF expansion.

KeyedHash.description: Keyed mode replaces the IV with the 32-byte key and sets KEYED_HASH flag on every compression.

DeriveKey.description: DeriveKey mode: context is hashed with DERIVE_KEY_CONTEXT flag to produce a 32-byte chaining value, then key material is hashed with DERIVE_KEY_MATERIAL flag using that as the IV.
DeriveKey.effect: Bug if either flag is missed or mixed up.

HexByteOrder.description: Bytes are asymmetric; reversed byte order would produce a different result.

HexNibbleOrder.description: Each byte has distinct high and low nibbles.

Pseudorandom.description: Standard correctness check on output value.

tests.comment: BLAKE3 of the empty message, 32-byte output. Spec KAT.
BLAKE3 of 'abc', 32-byte output. Short single-chunk KAT.
BLAKE3 of the empty message, 64-byte output. Empty extended to two output blocks; tests output-stream expansion past the first 32 bytes.
1-byte output. Smallest valid output. The output-stream first byte is byte 0 of the first 64-byte output block.
Output length 65 bytes. Forces a second output-block generation with counter=1; catches impls that don't advance the output counter or always return one block.
Output length 1024 bytes. Forces 16 output-block generations; sustained output-counter advancement.
Single-byte input. Smallest non-empty. Single block in a single chunk; the only compression call sets CHUNK_START | CHUNK_END | ROOT.
Input length block_size (64 bytes). Single full block in a single chunk; CHUNK_START and CHUNK_END are on the same compression.
Input length block_size+1 (65 bytes). Two-block chunk: first has CHUNK_START, second has CHUNK_END | ROOT.
Input length chunk_size-1 (1023 bytes). Single chunk filled to within 1 byte; last block is partial.
Input length chunk_size (1024 bytes). Exactly one full chunk; the chunk's chaining value becomes the root output (no parent compressions).

Input length chunk_size+1 (1025 bytes). Two chunks: first full (counter 0), second 1-byte (counter 1). Triggers the tree path with a single parent node compression; catches impls that always stay single-chunk.
Input length 2*chunk_size (2048 bytes). Two full chunks; one parent compression with PARENT flag combines them into the root.

Input length chunk_size + 500 (1524 bytes). Second chunk has a partial last block (500 - 64*7 = 52 bytes in block 7). Tests CHUNK_END flag on a partial last block of a non-first chunk; differs from the 1025-byte (single-block last chunk) and 2048-byte (full-block last chunk) cases.
Input length 3*chunk_size (3072 bytes). Three chunks; tree is unbalanced (left subtree has 2 chunks, right has 1). Catches impls that assume a perfectly balanced tree.
Input length 4*chunk_size (4096 bytes). Four chunks; perfectly balanced tree with depth 2 (2 parents + 1 root).
Input length 8*chunk_size (8192 bytes). Eight chunks; balanced tree of depth 3. Strong stress on chunk counter and parent-chaining.
Keyed BLAKE3: 32-byte key, 3-byte msg, 32-byte output. Tests that the KEYED_HASH flag is mixed into compressions and the key replaces the IV.
Keyed BLAKE3 across a tree: 32-byte key, 2048-byte msg, 32-byte output. KEYED_HASH flag must be set on ALL chunk and parent compressions, not just the first.
BLAKE3 derive_key mode with the canonical spec context string and empty key material. Tests DERIVE_KEY_CONTEXT flag on context compression and DERIVE_KEY_MATERIAL on the material compression.

Multi-block context (1124 bytes), short key material. The context is itself hashed first (with DERIVE_KEY_CONTEXT flag set on all its chunk and parent compressions); catches impls that only flag the first context block.
Asymmetric message bytes; nibbles within each byte differ. Catches hex parser nibble-swap or byte-reverse bugs.
